summary: The five-plan replacement is executable against the current release/0.7.1 codebase and preserves the security, reliability, integrity, and supportability controls needed for Composer parity. No blocking issue or unresolved warning remains.
resolved_findings: Made proposal bases explicit and sealed guided proposals once against a preallocated checkpoint, with predecessor-drift rejection in atomic staging.
Kept one compose-lock owner and one atomic state, proposal, event, operation-result, and guided-metadata settlement boundary.
Added deterministic, idempotent inline custody with primary-key conflict verification and lease fencing.
Added durable operation identity, takeover fencing, and production POST /guided/start wiring for ordinary guided creation.
Made proposal supersession, reviewed-fact invalidation, revert races, and deferred-intent consumption fail closed.
Kept fork blob copying explicitly compensating, with cleanup and archived-child recovery instead of claiming filesystem transactionality.
Made deferred-intent coverage server-owned and mechanically verified over closed candidate predicates.
Added the missing frontend, deployment-document, tracked-eval, accessibility, and staging acceptance coverage.

reviewers.summary: Current backend, database, frontend, deployment-document, and test paths support the planned seams and production caller changes.
The plan has one canonical topology language, one proposal lifecycle, single-seal guided bases, and explicit ownership for deferred-intent coverage.
Concurrency, retry, redaction, cancellation, accessibility, fixture tracking, and repository guardrail coverage are concrete and testable.
Staging, acceptance, revert, fork, supersession, and operation-takeover transitions have explicit recovery and integrity behavior.

evidence_notes: Loomweave was refreshed to the reviewed baseline before plan drafting.
Warpline change-impact evidence was advisory and changed-only; live code and route inspection supplied the authoritative feasibility checks.
